body_txt: In Real Life , most lightning bolts are purplish white or bluish white . Sometimes they're reddish, orange, or even greenish. However, in certain types of works, lightning bolts and other types of electricity are chiefly yellow note Which can happen in real life , but when the air is dry. And since lightning is usually accompanied by rain, yellow is quite an uncommon color for lightning, at least when not having an orange tinge. , often a simple zigzag shape and seldom striking from the ground to sky or from the one to be shocked to the object. Justified since yellow is a standard warning sign color, associated with electricity and fire in general (and as we all know, lightning is electric). Plus, etymologically, the word "electric" came from an old Greek word for amber note ήλεκτρον, pronounced "electron" , a yellow gem that was known and used by ancient scientists for its electrostatic properties. Furthermore, due to the Sun appearing yellow when viewed from Earth note The Sun's actual color is white , yellow is associated with light, and so is lightning. Most video games with the Fire, Ice, Lightning trinity will also make lightning yellow to differentiate it from ice. In live-action films and more realistic video games, though, lightning bolts are still predominantly blue. However, sometimes it is very blue so that there is no white seen. Alternatively , purple can be added to the mix, but again it tends to be a very vibrant purple. In a manner, such purple lightning can clash well with the traditional yellow. A Sub-Trope of Color-Coded Elements .
